atDirectives.name: @tailwind
atDirectives.description: Use the @tailwind directive to insert Tailwind's base, components, utilities and variants styles into your CSS.

atDirectives.name: @apply
atDirectives.description: Use @apply to inline any existing utility classes into your own custom CSS.

atDirectives.name: @layer
atDirectives.description: Use the @layer directive to tell Tailwind which 'bucket' a set of custom styles belong to.

atDirectives.name: @config
atDirectives.description: Use @config to specify which Tailwind configuration file to use.

atDirectives.name: @theme
atDirectives.description: Use @theme to define custom theme values in Tailwind CSS v4.

atDirectives.name: @import
atDirectives.description: Use @import to import CSS files in Tailwind CSS.

atDirectives.name: @responsive
atDirectives.description: Use @responsive to generate responsive variants of custom styles.

atDirectives.name: @variants
atDirectives.description: Use @variants to generate variant versions of custom utilities.

pseudoClasses.name: hover
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when the user hovers over an element

pseudoClasses.name: focus
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when an element has focus

pseudoClasses.name: active
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when an element is being activated by the user

pseudoClasses.name: visited
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when a link has been visited

pseudoClasses.name: checked
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when a checkbox or radio button is checked

pseudoClasses.name: disabled
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when an element is disabled

pseudoClasses.name: first
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to the first child element

pseudoClasses.name: last
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to the last child element

pseudoClasses.name: odd
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to odd child elements

pseudoClasses.name: even
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to even child elements

pseudoClasses.name: first-child
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to the first child element

pseudoClasses.name: last-child
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to the last child element

pseudoClasses.name: nth-child
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to elements based on their position

pseudoClasses.name: sm
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles at the small breakpoint (640px)

pseudoClasses.name: md
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles at the medium breakpoint (768px)

pseudoClasses.name: lg
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles at the large breakpoint (1024px)

pseudoClasses.name: xl
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles at the extra large breakpoint (1280px)

pseudoClasses.name: 2xl
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles at the 2xl breakpoint (1536px)

pseudoClasses.name: dark
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles in dark mode

pseudoClasses.name: group
pseudoClasses.description: Marks a parent element for group-hover and group-focus styles

pseudoClasses.name: group-hover
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to child elements when the parent is hovered

pseudoClasses.name: group-focus
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to child elements when the parent has focus

pseudoClasses.name: group-active
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to child elements when the parent is active

pseudoClasses.name: peer
pseudoClasses.description: Marks a sibling element for peer-hover and peer-focus styles

pseudoClasses.name: peer-hover
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when the preceding sibling element is hovered

pseudoClasses.name: peer-focus
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when the preceding sibling element has focus

pseudoClasses.name: peer-active
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when the preceding sibling element is active

pseudoClasses.name: required
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to required form elements

pseudoClasses.name: valid
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to valid form elements

pseudoClasses.name: invalid
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to invalid form elements

pseudoClasses.name: in-range
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when a form element's value is within the specified range

pseudoClasses.name: out-of-range
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles when a form element's value is outside the specified range

pseudoClasses.name: placeholder
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to placeholder text

pseudoClasses.name: file
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to file input elements

pseudoClasses.name: read-only
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to read-only form elements

pseudoClasses.name: open
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to details/summary elements when open

pseudoClasses.name: backdrop
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to the backdrop of an element (e.g., dialog)

pseudoClasses.name: selection
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to selected text

pseudoClasses.name: marker
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to list markers

pseudoClasses.name: before
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to the ::before pseudo-element

pseudoClasses.name: after
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to the ::after pseudo-element

pseudoClasses.name: first-letter
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to the first letter of a block-level element

pseudoClasses.name: first-line
pseudoClasses.description: Applies styles to the first line of a block-level element

pseudoElements.name: before
pseudoElements.description: Creates a pseudo-element before the element's content

pseudoElements.name: after
pseudoElements.description: Creates a pseudo-element after the element's content

pseudoElements.name: first-letter
pseudoElements.description: Styles the first letter of a block-level element

pseudoElements.name: first-line
pseudoElements.description: Styles the first line of a block-level element

pseudoElements.name: backdrop
pseudoElements.description: Styles the backdrop of dialog elements

pseudoElements.name: placeholder
pseudoElements.description: Styles placeholder text in form elements
